issue_title: torch.isin on GPU vs numpy isin: perf way slower than expected
surface_reasoning: Priority 3: labeled `module: performance`. Title states `torch.isin` on GPU is way slower than numpy isin — unexpected performance gap versus CPU/numpy equivalent strongly suggests dispatch selects a suboptimal GPU kernel for this op.
confidence_justification: Start: 0.50. Step 1: 'triaged' label present → +0.10. Running total: 0.60. Step 2: Partial repro signal — issue body contains measured benchmark numbers (25-43x slower than numpy) and an inline code snippet showing the isin builtin implementation, but the full executable repro is in an external gist, not self-contained in the issue body. Apply partial repro adjustment → +0.05. Running total: 0.65. Step 3: fix_scope is single_function (the torch.isin GPU kernel dispatch path, identified at aten/src/ATen/native/cuda/TensorCompare.cpp) → +0.10. Running total: 0.75. Step 4: No maintainer comments at all — zero COLLABORATOR/MEMBER/OWNER engagement. The only comment is from a NONE-association user confirming they observe the same slowness. Absence of maintainer engagement on a 2-year-old issue reduces confidence. No formal penalty in rubric but this is a missing positive signal. Step 5: No 'module: correctness (silent)' label — performance issue only, no +0.25 correctness bonus. Step 6: Issue ~2 years old but no confirmed maintainer activity, so open-2-years bonus +0.05 does not clearly apply (the staleness suggests no one intends to fix it soon) — apply a conservative -0.05 for lack of maintainer engagement beyond the triage label. Running total: 0.70. Final (clamped): 0.70.
ground_truth_delta: Unlike ambiguous_72408, there is no maintainer_blocked_action signal here — no one has said the fix is infeasible or requires external libraries. The issue has a concrete performance gap (25-43x slower than NumPy) with benchmark numbers and a triaged label. It is more actionable than 72408 because no blocking signals exist. Less actionable than positive_71774 because it is a performance/missing-fast-path issue (not silent correctness), has zero maintainer engagement beyond the triage label, and has no attempted fix PR. The lack of maintainer comments is the key differentiator — it is unclear whether this is considered low priority or simply overlooked.
